title: Time-ordered CatBoost expert with has_time and ordered boosting
summary: CatBoost explicitly supports preserving sample order with `has_time`, avoiding random permutations during categorical transformation and tree building.
rationale: CatBoost explicitly supports preserving sample order with `has_time`, avoiding random permutations during categorical transformation and tree building. For this task, rows are naturally chronological within race, so a strictly time-ordered CatBoost can be a materially different expert from the current shuffled grouped-CV CatBoost.
implementation_hint: Sort each fold by true chronology such as Year, Race, LapNumber, Driver, then train a separate CatBoost expert with `has_time=True`, ordered boosting settings, and no globally precomputed encodings. Evaluate with fold-level forward ordering and keep it as a standalone model or stack input rather than forcing it into the same feature pipeline as XGB.
expected_effect: Can reduce prediction shift on high-cardinality categorical structure and produce a decorrelated expert for ensembling.
risk: Needs careful within-fold sorting; if chronology is mis-specified, performance can drop sharply.
